evals.name: review-redundant-outgoing-query-assertions
evals.prompt:
Review these specs and tell me which assertions should be removed or changed and why:

```ruby
RSpec.describe Tasks::AssignCommand do
  let(:task) { create(:task) }
  let(:user) { create(:user) }
  subject(:command) { described_class.new(task, user) }

  describe "#perform" do
    it "updates the assignee" do
      command.perform
      expect(task.reload.assignee).to eq(user)
    end

    it "enqueues the notification job" do
      expect { command.perform }.to have_enqueued_job(NotificationJob).with(task)
    end

    it "increments the assignee count" do
      command.perform
      expect(task.assignees.count).to eq(1)
    end

    it "sets the assignee name" do
      command.perform
      expect(task.assignee.full_name).to eq(user.full_name)
    end

    it "responds to full_name" do
      expect(user).to respond_to(:full_name)
    end
  end
end
```
evals.expected_output: Identifies the redundant outgoing-query assertions (assignee count, assignee full_name) as owned by receiver specs. Catches the respond_to tautology. Explains the message-direction reasoning. Correctly identifies the two good tests as keepers.

assertions.text: Identifies 'assignees.count' assertion as redundant / owned by the Task model spec
Identifies 'assignee.full_name' assertion as redundant / owned by the User model spec
Identifies 'respond_to(:full_name)' as tautological (tests presence not behavior)
Correctly identifies the two GOOD tests ('updates the assignee', 'enqueues the notification job') as keepers
Explains the receiver-owns-assertion principle (not just says 'delete it')

evals.name: write-service-spec-with-collaborators
evals.prompt:
Write specs for this service class. Be thorough about what to test and what to skip.

```ruby
class Contacts::ArchiveService
  def initialize(contact, current_user)
    @contact = contact
    @current_user = current_user
  end

  def call
    return false if @contact.open_tasks.any?

    @contact.archive!
    AuditLog.create!(user: @current_user, action: :archive, subject: @contact)
    ContactArchivedJob.perform_later(@contact)
    true
  end
end
```
evals.expected_output: Writes specs that: test return value (true/false), assert contact state change, assert job enqueued, test the open_tasks guard. Does NOT assert internal AuditLog attributes. Does NOT mock Contact or Task domain objects.

assertions.text: Tests the false/guard path when open tasks exist
Tests that contact is archived via state change (not mocking archive!)
Does NOT mock Contact or Task domain objects (uses real FactoryBot records)
Does NOT assert internal AuditLog attributes inside this spec

evals.name: tdd-scope-with-exclusion-side
evals.prompt:
TDD a scope on this model. The scope is called `.active` and should return contacts where archived_at is nil and account matches the given account. Write the spec first, then the implementation.

```ruby
class Contact < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :account
  # archived_at: datetime, nullable
end
```
evals.expected_output: Follows red-green-refactor. Scaffolds empty scope before first test. Includes both matching AND non-matching records (archived contact, cross-account contact). Uses contain_exactly. Writes the test before the implementation.

assertions.text: Writes the failing spec BEFORE writing the implementation
Scope test includes a non-matching record (archived contact) to prove exclusion
Scope test includes a cross-account contact to prove account scoping
Uses contain_exactly or equivalent (not just include, which can't prove exclusion)
Scaffolds the empty scope method before the first test run (avoiding NameError red)
